title: NIS2 Human Resources Security ‚Äî Joiner/Mover/Leaver Process

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(i) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) (Human resources and access control) is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.2.14: NIS2 Human Resources Security ‚Äî Joiner/Mover/Leaver Process.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.14' running on sourcetype WinEventLog:Security and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

dataSources: `index=hr` `sourcetype=hr:jml_events` via HEC (fields employee_id, event_type, termination_date, job_change_id); `index=auth` `sourcetype=azure:aad:signin` with Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services ([Splunkbase 3110](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3110)); `index=windows` `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`; `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:task` (deprovision tickets) via Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)).
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110)
spl:
| inputlookup terminated_employees.csv
| eval term_date=strptime(termination_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_since_term=round((now()-term_date)/86400, 0)
| join type=left user [
    search index=auth OR index=windows OR index=azure earliest=-30d
    | eval user=coalesce(Account_Name, userPrincipalName, user)
    | stats latest(_time) as last_auth count as auth_count by user
]
| where isnotnull(last_auth) AND last_auth > term_date
| eval days_active_after_term=round((last_auth - term_date)/86400, 0)
| sort - days_active_after_term
| table user, termination_date, days_since_term, days_active_after_term, auth_count
description: Joins authoritative HR termination dates from `terminated_employees.csv` (or HEC `hr:jml_events`) against subsequent authentication activity drawn from Entra ID, Windows, and shared auth indexes to spotlight accounts still authenticating after the leaver timestamp.
value: Provisioning audits rarely fail on paper‚Äîthey fail when old accounts keep logging in because a deletion script missed a shadow admin; giving the privacy officer and risk committee a dated trail of post-termination authentications closes that evidentiary gap ahead of regulator questions.
implementation: (1) HR publishes `terminated_employees.csv` nightly with `user` UPN + `termination_date`. (2) Map `index=hr` feed for near-real-time events. (3) Alert if `days_active_after_term>0`. (4) Severity++ if >3 days. (5) Auto-task ServiceNow `RITM` via alert. (6) Store signed exports with `evidenceSigning`.
visualization: Timeline of last_auth vs term_date, gauge of open violations, ServiceNow ticket drilldown.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user
knownFalsePositives: 1) **Shared mailbox or role account renamed** so HR CSV still lists old `user` string while authentication uses new alias. *Distinguish:* Azure `userPrincipalName` change logs in **Entra ID > Audit**. *Suppress:* lookup `nis2_account_alias_map.csv`. 2) **Contractor extensions** recorded late in HRIS. *Distinguish:* `event_type=rehire_pending` in `hr:jml_events`. *Suppress:* time-bound exception register until HR closes ticket. 3) **Offline laptop logging cached credentials** days after term but never hits network until imaging. *Distinguish:* `last_auth` older than 72h yet after term. *Suppress:* optional `where days_active_after_term>3` for paging only. 4) **ServiceNow deprovision automation re-enabling SaaS sandbox accounts** for knowledge transfer. *Distinguish:* `RITM` contains `re_enable_for_kt`. *Suppress:* `lookup nis2_kt_exceptions user`. 5) **Timezone-aligned termination at midnight UTC vs local HR calendar**. *Distinguish:* HR provides `termination_timezone`. *Suppress:* adjust `term_date` with `strptime` + offset.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Authoritative HR feed: either CSV (`terminated_employees.csv`) dropped into `lookups/` with ACL `hr_compliance` read-only, or HEC to `index=hr` `sourcetype=hr:jml_events` containing `termination_date`, `employee_id`, `user`, `event_type`.
‚Ä¢ Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services ([Splunkbase 3110](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3110)) ensuring Entra sign-ins include `userPrincipalName` for correlation.
‚Ä¢ Splunk Add-on for Windows ([Splunkbase 742](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)) for Domain Controller `WinEventLog:Security` success codes 4624/4768.
‚Ä¢ Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) optional for validating `RITM` closure states.
‚Ä¢ Legal basis documented: DPIA stating Splunk processes HR identifiers under Art.6(1)(c) GDPR.

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
‚Ä¢ HR job writes CSV with columns `user,termination_date,reason_code` daily at 01:00 local; Splunk heavy forwarder rsync pulls to `lookups/` then `splunk reload lookup-table` via REST.
‚Ä¢ For HEC, props.conf sets `TIME_FORMAT=%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z` on `hr:jml_events`.
‚Ä¢ Validate counts: `| inputlookup terminated_employees.csv | stats count` matches Workday export row totals.
‚Ä¢ ServiceNow TA: enable `snow:task` input filtering `short_description=*depropvision*` (tune query).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Use `spl` field; add `| where days_active_after_term>0` alert daily 06:00.
Alternate streaming variant replacing CSV with `index=hr event_type=termination` generating identical fields via `rename`.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
‚Ä¢ For synthetic user, disable account in AD and confirm final Windows logon timestamp aligns (+/‚àí5 min) with Splunk `last_auth`.
‚Ä¢ Cross-check Azure portal **Revoke sessions** action timestamps.
‚Ä¢ Legal sample: request random ex-employee folder ‚Äî compare Splunk evidence CSV with HR PDF letter date.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
‚Ä¢ Dashboard with red/yellow thresholds (0 days = info, ‚â•3 days = critical).
‚Ä¢ Integrate SOAR to call **Microsoft Graph /users/{id}/invalidateAllRefreshTokens** (external runbook).
‚Ä¢ Quarterly evidence pack signer references `evidenceSigning` manifest.

Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting
‚Ä¢ **join drops everything** ‚Äî field case mismatch; enforce lower(`user`).
‚Ä¢ **Mass false positives after domain migration** ‚Äî refresh CSV UPNs.
‚Ä¢ **subsearch limits** ‚Äî switch to `| map` or summary index `summary_jml`.
‚Ä¢ **Azure misses last_auth** ‚Äî verify `index=azure` alias; add `index=auth` to subsearch.
grandmaExplanation: When someone stops working for us, we check the electronic keys still are not used afterward. If a former employee‚Äôs name still shows up logging in after their last day, we treat that as urgent housekeeping.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable for Authentication (optional acceleration for 30d windows)
exclusions: Does not execute HR disciplinary processes, notify labor councils, decide rehire eligibility, or replace court-grade HRIS extracts when employment law mandates certified paper files.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.14` with hashed identifiers, companion `hr:jml_events` excerpts proving termination_date provenance, ServiceNow RITM closure IDs, and TSA countersign files per the corporate evidence-signing standard for timestamped archives.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Controlled test user `jdoe@corp` receives `termination_date=yesterday` in `terminated_employees.csv` while a scripted Entra sign-in occurs today; the join surfaces `days_active_after_term=1` and nonzero `auth_count`, demonstrating detection of lingering access.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Same user after Global Admin runs `Revoke sessions` shows no `azure:aad:signin` or Windows success events for thirty days, HR adds `event_type=rehire_cancelled` row, and the search returns empty while HRIS attestation PDF is uploaded manually for auditors.
